entries.source:
Chat Edit: 'Excellent work! 🎉 You've successfully completed a comprehensive VS Code extension project with all the essential components in place.

Summary of Achievements
✅ Complete Extension Setup

Extension Development Host configurations for both AI-enabled and testing modes

Proper LSP server integration with IPC transport

Local AI completions for PowerShell, JavaScript, and TypeScript

36+ compiler integrations accessible via commands

✅ Professional Documentation

Comprehensive README with quick start, features, and architecture

Complete installation and usage instructions

Troubleshooting guide and performance metrics

Development workflow documentation

✅ Ready for Development

Working npm scripts for compile, watch, package, and test

Proper dependency management (Node.js built-ins + LSP libraries)

Clean project structure with all necessary configuration files

Next Steps Available
Your extension is now ready for:

Testing: Press F5 to launch Extension Development Host

Development: Use npm run watch for live development

Distribution: Use npm run package to create VSIX for sharing

Enhancement: Add more language models or compiler integrations

The foundation is solid and extensible. Great job building a feature-rich VS Code extension with local AI capabilities! 🚀

Its not supposed to be an extension its supposed to be for my IDE

D:\MyCopilot-IDE

I understand now - you're building your own IDE called "MyCopilot-IDE", not a VS Code extension. Let me check the current project structure to see what needs to be adjusted.

Allow read-only tools outside your workspace
Allowed
I need permission to list directories outside the workspace.
D:\MyCopilot-IDE

Allow read-only tools outside your workspace

Allow
I need permission to list directories outside the workspace.
D:\MyCopilot-IDE'
